title: Complex EML Branch Cut Atlas

principal_log_cuts.name: Principal Log(z): negative real axis
principal_log_cuts.discontinuity: Im(Log) jumps by 2π across the cut
principal_log_cuts.impact_on_ceml: ceml(z1, z2) = exp(z1) − Log(z2); discontinuous in z2 along negative real axis

principal_log_cuts.name: ceml(0, z) = 1 − Log(z)
principal_log_cuts.impact_on_ceml: Single-node EML inherits Log's cut directly

principal_log_cuts.name: ceml(z, c) = exp(z) − Log(c)  (c constant)
principal_log_cuts.cut: none in z — exp is entire; cut only if c < 0
principal_log_cuts.discontinuity: entire in z when c is fixed off the cut
principal_log_cuts.impact_on_ceml: First argument slot: no cut (exp is entire)

principal_log_cuts.name: ceml(c, z) = exp(c) − Log(z)  (c constant)
principal_log_cuts.impact_on_ceml: Second argument slot inherits Log cut

principal_log_cuts.name: Depth-2: ceml(ceml(a, b), 1)
principal_log_cuts.cut: b on negative real axis → inner ceml discontinuous → exp of discontinuous → outer exp discontinuous
principal_log_cuts.discontinuity: propagated discontinuity (multiplied by outer exp derivative)

principal_log_cuts.name: Depth-2: ceml(z, ceml(a, b))
principal_log_cuts.cut: outer Log adds another cut: when exp(a)−Log(b) hits negative real axis
principal_log_cuts.discontinuity: compound cut: depends on a, b jointly
principal_log_cuts.impact_on_ceml: Nested structure creates compound branch cuts

compound_cut_test.compound_cut_description: ceml(a, ceml(0,z)) has a compound cut at z=e where inner = 0

monodromy.branch_point: z2 = 0 in depth-2: ceml(z1, ceml(z3, z4))
monodromy.monodromy: inner ceml shifts by −2πi; outer Log(inner) then picks up monodromy of inner
monodromy.ceml_effect: compound shift depending on derivative of outer Log

monodromy.monodromy: exp(z1) is entire; no monodromy in z1 slot

safe_domains.condition: c not on negative real axis

safe_domains.safe_domain: ℂ \ (−∞, 0]  (z not on neg real axis)
safe_domains.condition: z not zero or negative real
z not on negative real axis or zero

safe_domains.safe_domain: where inner ceml ∉ (−∞, 0] and inner defined
safe_domains.condition: compound: inner must be off cut AND positive real part > 0

safe_domains.safe_domain: ℂ \ {z3 on cut}  — outer input is exp(inner) > 0 always
safe_domains.condition: outer second arg z3 off cut; inner always gives positive real for certain inputs

key_findings: F1: ceml(z1, z2) is entire in z1 (exp slot) — first slot never creates cuts
F2: ceml(z1, z2) has principal Log's cut in z2 — second slot inherits {Im(z2)=0, Re(z2)≤0}
F3: ceml(z, 1) = exp(z) — entirely entire, zero cuts anywhere
F4: Compound depth-2 cuts appear at unexpected points (e.g., z=e for ceml(a, ceml(0,z)))
F5: Monodromy: encircling z2=0 shifts ceml output by −2πi
F6: The imaginary-input path ceml(ix,1) is everywhere continuous (trig functions)
theorems: T1 (Branch-Free First Slot): For any ceml tree, the exp-slot child contributes no branch cuts
T2 (Cut Propagation): A cut in depth-k subtree propagates multiplicatively through composition
T3 (Entire Trig): ceml(i·f(x), 1) is entire for any entire f, giving sin/cos/exp of complex args
T4 (Compound Cut Location): ceml(c, ceml(0,z)) has a cut at z=e (where inner=0)
